mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in PAYEMS raises household income expectations and lowers perceived recession risk, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when payroll growth remains above zero.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with positive 3-month PAYEMS growth do not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than periods with nonpositive growth over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp upward crossing in FEDFUNDS tightens discount rates faster than earnings expectations adjust, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days after the policy-rate shock.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.50pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth falls below CPIAUCSL growth, real retail demand is contracting despite nominal sales, reducing expected revenues and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month RSAFS growth minus 3-month CPIAUCSL growth crosses below 0
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction signal has nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 forward returns during triggered periods or Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 6-month trend in INDPRO signals improving production volumes and operating leverage, raising expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 6-month INDPRO trends are not associated with higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than negative trends in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A large downward crossing in HOUST signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and future construction spillovers, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 15% year over year
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST year-over-year declines greater than 15% are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is low but FEDFUNDS is rising, tight labor markets increase wage-inflation pressure and force policy tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4.0% and FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint UNRATE-FEDFUNDS trigger does not underperform the unconditional 20-day S&P 500 return by at least 25 bps on average in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth confirms broad real demand expansion and supports earnings expectations, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days when real output growth is above zero.
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with positive GDPC1 growth do not produce higher subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns than quarters with nonpositive GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid CPIAUCSL acceleration raises expected policy restraint and equity discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days after the inflation surprise is observable.
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL acceleration triggers above 6% are followed by nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or negative hit rate is below 50%.
